This action will be a Firm Fixed-Price Contract to include software and associated success support with an estimated annual cost of $532,194 for a one (1) year base period with four (4) one-year option periods. The provider is a HUBZone small business set-aside directed to DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9), 161 Fort Evans Road NE, Suite 255 Leesburg VA 20176.

The solicitation proposes to award an Indefinite Quantity Contract (IQC) for:
• Posit Package Manager Advanced
• Posit Connect Advanced - 200 Licenses Package
• Posit Workbench Advanced - 20 Licenses Package
• Posit Success Support

DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9) is the only HUBZone small business source that is a Posit authorized distributor identified through market research that is capable of providing these part numbers. The contract will cover a Base Period of one year with four one year option periods for each item. The total estimated dollar value for the base year is $532,194.

The Government will make award to one HUBZone small business.

L.3.1. Exceptions: Offerors are required to meet all solicitation requirements, such as terms and conditions, representations and certifications, and technical requirements, in addition to those identified as evaluation factors. Failure to meet a requirement may result in an offer being ineligible for award. If the offeror finds it necessary to take exception to any of the requirements specified in this solicitation, the offeror shall clearly identify the applicable Volume and exceptions with a complete explanation of why the exception was taken, what benefit accrues to the Government (if any), and its impact, if any, on the performance, schedule, price, and specific requirements of the solicitation. Each exception shall be specifically related to each paragraph and/or specific part of the solicitation to which the exception is taken. Offerors are cautioned that taking an exception may render the offer ineligible for award.

IAW FAR 12 The Government intends to make a source selection as part of a HUBZone competitive set-aside utilizing the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) process. The LPTA process does not permit tradeoffs among price and non-price factors. Offerors shall assume that the Government has no prior knowledge of their facilities and experience. The Government will base its evaluation on the information presented in the Offeror's proposal.
Factor 1. Management/Technical Approach.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's management approach to determine if the proposed approach demonstrates an understanding of the need for a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce and will result in a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce capable of performing awarded TO requirements.

SECTION I – CONTRACT CLAUSES

The following Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) clauses are incorporated by reference and apply to this solicitation and any resulting contract:

52.219-3 Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award (NOV 2020)
52.219-4 Notice of Price Evaluation Preference for HUBZone Small Business Concerns (OCT 2022)
52.219-8 Utilization of Small Business Concerns (OCT 2022)
52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (OCT 2022)
52.219-28 Postaward Small Business Program Rerepresentation (MAR 2023)

This solicitation is issued as a total HUBZone small business set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1305. Only offers from HUBZone small business concerns that are certified by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) at the time of offer submission and that maintain their HUBZone certification through contract award will be considered for award. Offerors must be listed as a certified HUBZone small business concern in the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) at the time of initial offer and at the time of award.

The applicable NAICS code for this requirement is 561210 – Facilities Support Services, with a size standard of $47.0 million. The HUBZone price evaluation preference factor of 10 percent shall apply in accordance with FAR 19.1307 when evaluating offers from HUBZone small business concerns against offers from other than HUBZone small business concerns, should the set-aside be withdrawn. Contractors must comply with FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting, ensuring that at least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel shall be expended for employees of the HUBZone concern.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service, Southeast Area, anticipates awarding a single Firm Fixed-Price contract for facilities maintenance and custodial support services at the Jamie Whitten Delta States Research Center in Stoneville, Mississippi. The period of performance includes a 12-month base period and four 12-month option periods.

SOLICITATION NUMBER: W912EP-25-R-0043
ISSUING OFFICE: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Jacksonville District
SET-ASIDE: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside

1.0 DESCRIPTION

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Jacksonville District, is soliciting proposals from qualified HUBZone certified small business concerns for environmental remediation and restoration services at multiple Formerly Used Defense Sites (FUDS) throughout Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. This requirement is set aside exclusively for HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.13 and DFARS 219.13.

The contracting officer has conducted market research in accordance with FAR Part 10 and has determined there is a reasonable expectation that at least two responsible HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at fair market prices. This determination supports the total HUBZone set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1305(b).

2.0 CONTRACT TYPE AND ESTIMATED VALUE

The Government intends to award a single Indefinite-Delivery/Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ) contract with a guaranteed minimum of $250,000 and a maximum ceiling of $22,000,000 over a five-year ordering period. The applicable NAICS code is 562910 – Remediation Services, with a size standard of $25.0 million. Individual task orders will be issued on a Firm Fixed-Price or Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee basis depending on the nature of the work.

3.0 ELIGIBILITY

To be eligible for award, offerors must be certified as a HUBZone small business concern by the SBA at the time of offer submission and must represent their HUBZone status in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, is incorporated herein. Failure to maintain HUBZone certification may result in disqualification from award consideration.

JUSTIFICATION FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION
HUBZone Sole Source Award

1. Agency: Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 4, Pittsburgh, PA
2. Contracting Activity: Veterans Health Administration, VA Pittsburgh Healthcare System
3. Description of Action: Sole source award to a HUBZone small business concern for biomedical equipment preventive maintenance and repair services.
4. Authority: FAR 6.302-5(b)(5) – Set-asides for HUBZone small business concerns authorized under FAR 19.1306.

5. Basis for Sole Source Justification:

Pursuant to FAR 19.1306(a), a sole source HUBZone award may be made when the contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns. Market research, including review of SAM.gov registrations, SBA's HUBZone certification database, and prior procurement history, identified MedServ Solutions, Inc. (DUNS: 078451239, CAGE: 8JKP2), located at 2490 Sidney Street, Suite 300, Pittsburgh, PA 15203, as the only certified HUBZone small business concern in the local area with the requisite OEM certifications and trained personnel to service the specific biomedical equipment models currently installed at VA Pittsburgh Healthcare System.

The anticipated total contract value, including options, is $3,850,000, which falls below the $4.5 million threshold for non-manufacturing NAICS codes as required by FAR 19.1306(a)(2). The applicable NAICS code is 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance, with a size standard of $34.0 million.

MedServ Solutions, Inc. has previously performed similar work under Contract No. 36C24420C0087 and received satisfactory to exceptional performance ratings in the Contractor Performance Assessment Reporting System (CPARS). The firm has been determined to be a responsible contractor. The requirement is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under FAR Subpart 19.8.

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS, CONDITIONS, AND NOTICES TO OFFERORS

L.1 GENERAL INFORMATION

Solicitation No.: 70RCSA25R00000081
Agency: Department of Homeland Security, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
Title: Cybersecurity Assessment and Vulnerability Scanning Support Services
NAICS Code: 541512 – Computer Systems Design Services (Size Standard: $34.0 million)
Set-Aside: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside
Contract Type: Time-and-Materials with Not-to-Exceed Ceiling

L.2 ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

This procurement is a total HUBZone set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305. Proposals will only be accepted from concerns that are certified by the SBA as HUBZone small business concerns at the time of proposal submission, at the time of award, and throughout the duration of contract performance. Offerors shall include in their proposals a copy of their current HUBZone certification letter issued by the SBA or a screenshot from the SBA's HUBZone certification database confirming active status.

The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, applies to this solicitation. Additionally, FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting, requires that at least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel be expended for employees of the concern. The HUBZone price evaluation preference described at FAR 52.219-4 shall apply in the event a HUBZone concern's offer is evaluated against a non-HUBZone concern, should the set-aside be partially withdrawn.

L.3 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

The anticipated period of performance is a 12-month base period commencing on or about September 1, 2025, with three 12-month option periods and a six-month extension option pursuant to FAR 52.217-8. The estimated total contract value across all periods is $8,500,000.

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE / REQUEST FOR INFORMATION
Solicitation Number: 1333ND25SNB340019
Agency: Department of Commerce, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service
Subject: Marine Observation Buoy Refurbishment and Deployment Services

The National Weather Service (NWS) is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified HUBZone certified small business concerns capable of performing refurbishment, calibration, and at-sea deployment of marine meteorological observation buoys along the Gulf Coast region. This sources sought notice is issued in contemplation of a HUBZone small business set-aside under FAR Subpart 19.13.

The anticipated requirement includes the overhaul and sensor recalibration of approximately 35 Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) stations and 12 moored buoy platforms annually, including hull cleaning, anti-fouling treatment, battery replacement, data logger programming, and vessel-based deployment. Work will be performed at a contractor-furnished facility within 100 miles of the Gulf Coast and at sea locations as directed by task order.

The applicable NAICS code is 336611 – Ship Building and Repairing, with a small business size standard of 1,300 employees. The estimated contract value is $6,200,000 over a base year and three option years.

Responses to this notice should include: (1) company name, address, CAGE code, and UEI number; (2) confirmation of active HUBZone certification with SBA certification number; (3) a brief capability statement not to exceed five pages; and (4) documentation of past performance on similar marine instrumentation or vessel-based deployment contracts.

If a sufficient number of qualified HUBZone small business concerns respond and the contracting officer determines there is a reasonable expectation that at least two HUBZone concerns will submit competitive proposals at fair market prices, NOAA intends to issue the formal solicitation as a total HUBZone set-aside with the FAR 52.219-3 clause and applicable HUBZone price evaluation preference under FAR 52.219-4. Interested firms must respond no later than June 20, 2025, at 2:00 PM Eastern Time.

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS

L.1 GENERAL INFORMATION

Solicitation Number: 693JJ3-24-R-000417
Issuing Agency: Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), Office of Acquisition and Grants Management
NAICS Code: 541330 – Engineering Services
Small Business Size Standard: $25.5M
Product Service Code: R425 – Support: Professional – Engineering/Technical

This procurement is a 100% HUBZone Set-Aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305. Only offerors that are certified by the Small Business Administration (SBA) as HUBZone small business concerns are eligible to submit proposals. Offerors must be a certified HUBZone small business concern at the time of proposal submission and must maintain HUBZone certification through contract award. The contracting officer will verify HUBZone status through the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) prior to award.

The clause at FAR 52.219-13, Notice of Set-Aside of Orders (Nov 2020), is incorporated by reference. Additionally, the HUBZone price evaluation preference per FAR 19.1307 applies to this solicitation.

L.2 EVALUATION METHODOLOGY

The Government will evaluate proposals using a Best Value Tradeoff methodology. Proposals will be evaluated against the following factors: (1) Technical Capability, (2) Past Performance, and (3) Price. Technical Capability and Past Performance, when combined, are significantly more important than Price.

L.3 PROPOSAL SUBMISSION

Offerors shall submit proposals electronically via email to fhwa-acquisitions@dot.gov no later than 2:00 PM Eastern Time on April 18, 2025. Late submissions will be handled in accordance with FAR 15.208. All proposals shall consist of a Technical Volume (not to exceed 30 pages), a Past Performance Volume, and a Price Volume submitted as separate files.

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

Solicitation No.: 47QFCA-25-R-0009
Issuing Office: General Services Administration (GSA), Federal Acquisition Service, Region 4
Title: Cybersecurity Assessment and Authorization (A&A) Support Services
NAICS Code: 541512 – Computer Systems Design Services
Size Standard: $34.0M
PSC: D302 – IT and Telecom – Systems Development
Set-Aside Determination: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside
Contract Type: Time-and-Materials (T&M)
Anticipated Award: Single Award
Estimated Period of Performance: Base Period of 12 months, plus two (2) 12-month option periods

SECTION I – CONTRACT CLAUSES (Excerpt)

The following clauses are incorporated by reference (FAR 52.252-2):

FAR 52.219-3 – Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award (Oct 2022)
FAR 52.219-13 – Notice of Set-Aside of Orders (Nov 2020)
FAR 52.219-14 – Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)
FAR 52.219-28 – Post-Award Small Business Program Rerepresentation (Mar 2023)

The contractor must comply with the limitations on subcontracting requirements set forth in FAR 52.219-14 and 13 CFR 126.700. At least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel shall be expended for employees of the HUBZone small business concern or employees of other HUBZone small business concerns.

Offerors must be certified as a HUBZone small business concern by the U.S. Small Business Administration at the time of offer submission and must represent their HUBZone status in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). The Contracting Officer reserves the right to verify HUBZone eligibility through SAM.gov and SBA's certification database prior to evaluation and award.

DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
Network Contracting Office (NCO) 19 – Rocky Mountain Acquisition Center

COMBINED SYNOPSIS/SOLICITATION
Solicitation Number: 36C25925Q0312
Title: Medical Waste Disposal and Transportation Services – VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System
Classification Code: S222 – Waste Treatment and Storage
NAICS Code: 562112 – Hazardous Waste Collection
Size Standard: $47.0M
Type of Set-Aside: HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside
Type of Contract: Firm-Fixed-Price
Place of Performance: VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System, 1700 N Wheeling St, Aurora, CO 80045

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products and services prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a written solicitation will not be issued separately.

This requirement is being set aside 100% for HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR 19.1305 and VAAR 819.1305. The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award (Oct 2022), applies. A HUBZone price evaluation preference will be applied in accordance with FAR 19.1307 if applicable.

To be eligible for award, offerors must: (1) be certified as a HUBZone small business concern by the SBA and listed in SAM.gov at the time of offer; (2) remain certified through the date of award; and (3) provide evidence of all applicable state and federal permits for hazardous and regulated medical waste transportation and disposal.

The Government will award to the lowest price technically acceptable (LPTA) offeror whose proposal conforms to the solicitation requirements. Technical acceptability will be evaluated on a pass/fail basis. Questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted to Contract Specialist Maria T. Gonzalez at maria.gonzalez5@va.gov no later than March 3, 2025.

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES

B.1 GENERAL

This solicitation is issued as a HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305 and FAR 19.1307. Only concerns that are certified by the Small Business Administration (SBA) as HUBZone small business concerns are eligible to submit offers. The clause at FAR 52.219-13, Notice of Set-Aside of Orders, applies to this acquisition. Offerors must be a certified HUBZone small business concern at the time of offer submission and must remain certified through the date of contract award.

The applicable NAICS code for this procurement is 561210 – Facilities Support Services, with a small business size standard of $47.0 million in average annual receipts. The Product Service Code is S216, Housekeeping – Facilities Operations Support.

The U.S. General Services Administration (GSA), Public Buildings Service, Region 4, intends to award a single firm-fixed-price contract for comprehensive facilities maintenance and operations support services at the James H. Quillen Federal Building, located in Kingsport, Tennessee. The period of performance shall consist of one (1) base year and four (4) one-year option periods.

B.2 PRICE EVALUATION PREFERENCE

In accordance with FAR 19.1307, offers from HUBZone small business concerns will receive a price evaluation preference. When the lowest responsive offer is from a large business, the contracting officer shall apply a price evaluation factor of 10 percent to offers from non-HUBZone concerns for evaluation purposes only. This preference shall not apply to the actual contract award price.

B.3 ELIGIBILITY VERIFICATION

Prior to award, the Contracting Officer will verify the apparent successful offeror's HUBZone certification status through the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) database and SAM.gov. Failure to maintain active HUBZone certification may result in the offeror being deemed ineligible for award.

SOLICITATION NO. 75H71025R00038
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES
HEALTH RESOURCES AND SERVICES ADMINISTRATION (HRSA)

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS

L.1 SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION

This requirement is being procured as a total HUBZone set-aside pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.13 and FAR 19.1305(a). The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, is incorporated by reference. Only offerors that are SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns under the applicable NAICS code 541611 – Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services (size standard $24.5M) are eligible to submit proposals.

L.2 MARKET RESEARCH JUSTIFICATION

Market research conducted between September 2024 and January 2025 confirmed that there is a reasonable expectation that at least two responsible HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at fair market prices for the required program evaluation and technical assistance services in support of HRSA's Bureau of Primary Health Care. Responses to the Sources Sought Notice (No. 75H71025SNR0012) posted on SAM.gov on September 16, 2024, yielded capability statements from seven (7) SBA-certified HUBZone firms demonstrating relevant experience.

L.3 PROPOSAL SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS

Offerors shall submit proposals in two (2) separate volumes:
  Volume I – Technical Proposal (limited to 50 pages)
  Volume II – Price Proposal (no page limit)

Each offeror must include documentation of current HUBZone certification issued by the SBA, including the firm's certification date, certification number, and principal office address located within a designated HUBZone. Offerors must also certify that at least 35% of their employees reside in a HUBZone as required by 13 CFR 126.200.

AMENDMENT 0003 TO SOLICITATION W912DY-25-R-0044
U.S. ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS – SAVANNAH DISTRICT

PART I – SECTION A: SOLICITATION/CONTRACT FORM

A.1 This amendment is issued to: (1) respond to offeror questions received by the February 28, 2025 deadline, and (2) extend the proposal due date to March 21, 2025 at 2:00 PM EST.

A.2 SET-ASIDE STATUS

This solicitation remains a 100% HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside as stated in the original solicitation. The contracting officer has determined, in accordance with FAR 19.1305, that there is a reasonable expectation that offers will be received from two or more responsible HUBZone small business concerns and that award will be made at a fair market price. The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award (NOV 2020), is applicable and incorporated by full text in Section I of this solicitation.

A.3 QUESTION AND ANSWER LOG (EXCERPT)

Q7: Can a joint venture between a HUBZone firm and a non-HUBZone firm submit a proposal?
A7: Yes, provided the joint venture meets the requirements of 13 CFR 126.616. The HUBZone small business concern must be the managing venturer and perform at least 40% of the work. The joint venture must be SBA-certified as a HUBZone joint venture prior to proposal submission.

Q8: Is subcontracting to non-HUBZone firms permitted?
A8: Yes, however the HUBZone prime contractor must comply with the limitations on subcontracting requirements at FAR 52.219-14 and perform at least 50% of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel with its own employees for this service contract.

The applicable NAICS code remains 237990 – Other Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction, with a size standard of $45.0 million.

COMBINED SYNOPSIS/SOLICITATION
Department of Veterans Affairs
Network Contracting Office (NCO) 19 – Rocky Mountain Region
Solicitation Number: 36C25925Q0187

SUBJECT: Biomedical Equipment Preventive Maintenance and Repair Services – VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System

1.0 OVERVIEW

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial services prepared in accordance with FAR Subpart 12.6 and FAR 13.5, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

2.0 HUBZone SET-ASIDE

This procurement is set aside exclusively for HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR 19.1305 and Veterans Affairs Acquisition Regulation (VAAR) 819.1305. The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, applies to this acquisition. Only SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns may submit quotations.

The applicable NAICS code is 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance, with a small business size standard of $34.0 million.

3.0 EVALUATION METHODOLOGY

Award will be made to the responsible offeror whose quotation, conforming to the solicitation requirements, represents the best value to the Government considering the following factors: (1) Technical Capability, (2) Past Performance, and (3) Price. Technical Capability and Past Performance, when combined, are approximately equal to Price.

In addition, in accordance with FAR 19.1307 and the HUBZone price evaluation preference, offers from HUBZone small business concerns will be evaluated with a 10 percent price preference applied against non-HUBZone offers, should any such offers be considered in the competitive range.

4.0 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Base Year: July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026
Option Year 1: July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027
Option Year 2: July 1, 2027 – June 30, 2028

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE
National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)
National Weather Service – Office of Observations
Notice Number: 1305-AQ-25-00091-SS

The National Weather Service (NWS) is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified HUBZone small business concerns capable of providing calibration, maintenance, and logistics support services for the Automated Surface Observing System (ASOS) network across the continental United States and U.S. territories.

Based on the results of this sources sought notice, NOAA intends to issue a solicitation as a HUBZone small business set-aside under FAR Subpart 19.13 if a sufficient number of capable HUBZone concerns are identified. Specifically, the Contracting Officer must determine, pursuant to FAR 19.1305(a), that there is a reasonable expectation that at least two responsible HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers and that award can be made at a fair market price. If sufficient HUBZone firms are not identified, the Government may consider alternative set-aside strategies, including small business set-aside under FAR 19.502-2.

The anticipated NAICS code is 334519 – Other Measuring and Controlling Device Manufacturing, with a size standard of 500 employees. The estimated contract value is between $15 million and $25 million over a five-year ordering period (one base year plus four option years).

RESPONDENTS MUST PROVIDE THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION:
(a) Company name, DUNS/UEI number, CAGE code, and point of contact.
(b) Current SBA HUBZone certification status, including certification number and date of certification. Respondents should provide a screenshot or printout from the SBA's HUBZone certification database.
(c) Demonstration of relevant experience with meteorological or environmental monitoring equipment maintenance within the past five (5) years.
(d) Description of the firm's technical workforce and geographic coverage capability.

This notice is for market research purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation or commitment by the Government. No solicitation document exists at this time. Responses are due no later than April 4, 2025, 5:00 PM EDT.

C.3 VAAR 852.219-73 VA NOTICE OF TOTAL SET-ASIDE FOR CERTIFIED SERVICE-DISABLED VETERAN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESSES (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION)

(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses...

(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

PRESOLICITATION NOTICE – Initial Outfitting, Transition, and Activation Services in support of the San Marcos, TX CBOC

100% SDVOSB SET ASIDE

This is a Pre-Solicitation Notice that the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) intends to solicit quotes to provide non-personal Initial Outfitting, Transition, and Activation (IOT&A) services for the San Marcos, TX Community Based Outpatient Clinic (CBOC). This requirement supports VHA's mission by providing contractor support services to help bring the hospital into full planned operations that will provide health care services to Veterans. This will be a firm-fixed-price contract with an estimated Period of Performance of 33-months.

This requirement will be set-aside 100% for Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs) using FAR Part 12 and 37. To be eligible for an SDVOSB set aside under VA's Veterans First Contracting Program, SDVOSBs must:

be verified by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) as SDVOSB (see https://search.certifications.sba.gov/ for details)

and

meet the small business size standard for NAICS 541614.

This is also known as Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) Program (FAR 19.14). This is a notice of intent to negotiate a firm-fixed price (FFP) with a base performance period of one (1) year, and two (2) optional period of performance consisting of six (6) months each for a total possible performance period of two (2) years. The intent of award is to sole-source award to the incumbent under the previously reference authority. This is NOT a request for competitive quotations. However, contractors who want to challenge this action must submit an interest letter and a capabilities statement that demonstrates their ability to perform this work. The information must demonstrate the organization's ability to provide the product(s) and service(s) as described within this notice, as well as the associated justification. All interested organizations must respond to this Special Notice by March 19, 2026, 10:00 AM (MT), to the identified PoC's E-Mail (shawn.borisow@nist.gov). Area of Interest 1: Air Mobility Command (AMC) A6 – Base Information Technology Modernization – Please note that responses will only be accepted from Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs)

Introduction & Mission Objective
Air Mobility Command (AMC) seeks innovative partners to lead a comprehensive transformation of its Information Technology (IT) infrastructure. The current legacy environment is outdated, inefficient, and hinders the "Global Reach" mission by compromising operational speed, data sharing, and overall warfighter effectiveness.

The objective is to overhaul this system by assessing existing architecture, developing a forward-looking roadmap, and integrating modern technologies across core, cloud, and edge environments. The goal is to field a secure, scalable, and purpose-built IT infrastructure that enables data-driven, globally maneuverable airpower for modern joint warfare.

252.219-7009 Section 8(a) Direct Award.
SECTION 8(a) DIRECT AWARD (OCT 2018)
(a) This contract is issued as a direct award between the contracting office and the 8(a) Contractor pursuant to the Partnership Agreement between the Small Business Administration (SBA) and the Department of Defense. Accordingly, the SBA, even if not identified in Section A of this contract, is the prime contractor and retains responsibility for 8(a) certification, for 8(a) eligibility determinations and related issues, and for providing counseling and assistance to the 8(a) Contractor under the 8(a) Program.
(b) The contracting office is responsible for administering the contract and for taking any action on behalf of the Government under the terms and conditions of the contract; provided that the contracting office shall give advance notice to the SBA before it issues a final notice terminating performance, either in whole or in part, under the contract. The contracting office also shall coordinate with the SBA prior to processing any novation agreement. The contracting office may assign contract administration functions to a contract administration office.
(c) The 8(a) Contractor agrees that it will notify the Contracting Officer, simultaneous with its notification to the SBA (as required by SBA's 8(a) regulations at 13 CFR 124.515), when the owner or owners upon whom 8(a) eligibility is based plan to relinquish ownership or control of the concern.

These processes are tailored and maintained by the individual program management office of each capability. Within these processes, configuration management (CM), release management (RM), and testing activities are used to track and verify the changes to the operational baselines and ensure the changes meet requirements. This task order provides support within SE3 for testing, CM, and RM. The initial strategy is to support the Strategic Knowledge Integration Web SKIWeb, Storage as a Service (STaaS), Enterprise Messaging (EM), and Enterprise Privileged User Access Service (EPAUS) programs. 541512 - Computer Systems Design Services. Solicitation No. 36C25525R0047
Subject: Health Information Technology (HIT) Systems Modernization Support Services

This requirement is set aside exclusively for participants in the Small Business Administration (SBA) 8(a) Business Development Program pursuant to FAR 19.805 and FAR 52.219-11, Notice of Total 8(a) Set-Aside. The SBA has accepted this requirement for the 8(a) program under SBA acceptance letter dated 03/12/2025. The NAICS code for this acquisition is 541512 – Computer Systems Design Services, with a size standard of $34.0 million.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Veterans Health Administration (VHA) requires contractor support for the continued modernization and sustainment of electronic health record interfaces, clinical decision support tools, and data migration services across VISN 15 facilities located in Kansas City, MO; Wichita, KS; and Leavenworth, KS. The contractor shall provide systems engineering, software development, integration testing, and Tier 2/3 help desk support in accordance with the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS).

Contract Type: Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP) Task Order
Period of Performance: One (1) Base Year plus Four (4) Option Years
Estimated Value: $8.2M (total including options)
Place of Performance: Kansas City, MO (with potential remote work authorized per facility policy)
Security Clearance: Public Trust / Moderate Risk (MBI)

All responsible 8(a) certified small business concerns are encouraged to submit proposals. Offerors must be certified as 8(a) participants at the time of offer submission and through the duration of contract performance. The Contracting Officer will verify 8(a) eligibility through SAM.gov and the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS). Offers received from firms not certified under the 8(a) Business Development Program will not be considered for award. Questions regarding this solicitation shall be directed to Contracting Officer Maria T. Gallegos at maria.gallegos2@va.gov no later than April 4, 2025, 2:00 PM CT.

Pre-Solicitation Notice W9126G-25-R-0083
Geospatial Information Systems (GIS) and Environmental Data Management Support

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Fort Worth District, intends to award a competitive 8(a) set-aside contract for Geospatial Information Systems (GIS) and Environmental Data Management Support services. This acquisition is being conducted as an 8(a) competitive set-aside under the authority of FAR 19.805-2 and FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting. The applicable NAICS code is 541370 – Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services, with a small business size standard of $19.0 million.

Scope of Work: The contractor shall provide comprehensive GIS analysis, database development, environmental site characterization mapping, LIDAR data processing, geospatial web application development, and cartographic production services supporting USACE Civil Works and Military Programs across the Southwestern Division area of responsibility. Work includes, but is not limited to, ESRI ArcGIS Enterprise platform administration, spatial data quality assurance, hydrologic and hydraulic model visualization, and integration of geospatial data into the Corps' Common Operating Picture (COP) environment.

This procurement is being offered to 8(a) certified firms on a competitive basis. The SBA has accepted this requirement into the 8(a) Business Development Program (SBA Case No. FW-2025-0194). Only firms currently holding valid 8(a) certification from the SBA at time of proposal submission are eligible to compete. Joint ventures may participate provided they meet the requirements of 13 CFR 124.513.

Minimum Guarantee: $150,000

B.1 AUTHORITY AND SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION

This task order is issued under the GSA 8(a) STARS III Governmentwide Acquisition Contract (GWAC), which is reserved exclusively for certified 8(a) small business concerns. This order constitutes an 8(a) sole source award to Meridian Systems Group, LLC, an SBA-certified 8(a) participant (SBA Case No. 8A-2024-03891), pursuant to FAR 19.805-1 and in accordance with the Partnership Agreement between SBA and GSA.

The Small Business Administration has authorized this procurement as an 8(a) sole source action, and by execution of this task order, SBA agrees to furnish the required services through subcontract with the above-named 8(a) concern in accordance with FAR 52.219-11, Special 8(a) Contract Conditions, and FAR 52.219-17, Section 8(a) Award.

B.2 SCOPE OF WORK SUMMARY

The Contractor shall provide enterprise IT modernization support services including cloud migration planning, Zero Trust Architecture implementation, and Authority to Operate (ATO) documentation for HHS OCIO. Services include systems engineering, DevSecOps pipeline development, and Tier II/III application support for three (3) mission-critical platforms.

B.3 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Base Period: April 1, 2025 – March 31, 2026
Option Period 1: April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2027

AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION / MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT

Solicitation No.: W911QX-25-R-0033
Issuing Activity: U.S. Army Contracting Command – Aberdeen Proving Ground (ACC-APG)
Title: Cybersecurity Operations Center (CSOC) Analyst Support Services

A. ACQUISITION STRATEGY

This procurement is conducted as an 8(a) competitive set-aside in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.8 and the Partnership Agreement between the U.S. Small Business Administration and the Department of the Army. Only concerns currently certified under the SBA 8(a) Business Development Program are eligible to submit proposals. The SBA accepted this requirement into the 8(a) program on November 8, 2024 (SBA Acceptance Reference No. 8A-APG-2024-0177).

NAICS Code: 541519 – Other Computer Related Services
Product Service Code: D302 – IT and Telecom – Systems Development
Contract Type: Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee (CPFF), Level of Effort
Anticipated Award: Single Award
Estimated Value: $7,200,000 (base plus options)

B. APPLICABLE CLAUSES – 8(a) PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS

The following FAR clauses are incorporated and apply to this solicitation:

52.219-11 Special 8(a) Contract Conditions (DEV) (Sep 2023)
52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)
52.219-18 Notification of Competition Limited to Eligible 8(a) Participants (Oct 2022)

Offerors must maintain their 8(a) certification through the date of initial offer and through the date of award. In accordance with 13 CFR 124.507(d), if an 8(a) participant's program term expires after offer but before award, the firm remains eligible for award. However, option exercise is contingent upon the contractor's continued eligibility status or applicable SBA regulatory provisions.

C. LIMITATIONS ON SUBCONTRACTING

Pursuant to FAR 52.219-14, the 8(a) prime contractor must perform at least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel using its own employees. Proposals must include a subcontracting plan narrative and cost breakdown demonstrating compliance with this requirement.

SOURCES SOUGHT / REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI)

Agency: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
Network Contracting Office 4 (NCO 4) – Philadelphia, PA
Notice Number: 36C24425SNT0091
Posted Date: March 3, 2025
Response Deadline: March 21, 2025, 2:00 PM EST

TITLE: Medical Facility Janitorial and Custodial Services – Coatesville VA Medical Center

NAICS Code: 561720 – Janitorial Services
Size Standard: $22.0 Million
Product Service Code: S201 – Housekeeping Services

DESCRIPTION:

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 4, is conducting market research to identify qualified 8(a) certified small business concerns capable of providing comprehensive janitorial, custodial, and infection control cleaning services at the Coatesville VA Medical Center, Coatesville, PA 19320.

The previous contract (36C244-20-D-0083) was awarded as an 8(a) sole source action to ProClean Services, Inc. (SBA-certified 8(a) participant), with a period of performance ending June 30, 2025. The VA is now evaluating whether this follow-on requirement should be procured as an 8(a) competitive set-aside, 8(a) sole source under FAR 19.805-1(a)(2), or an unrestricted full and open competition.

In order to determine whether an 8(a) set-aside is appropriate, the Government requires responses from at least two (2) 8(a)-certified small businesses demonstrating the requisite experience, capability, and capacity to perform these services. Respondents must clearly identify their SBA 8(a) certification status, 8(a) program entry and graduation dates, and provide evidence of relevant past performance on contracts of similar size, scope, and complexity.

All interested 8(a) firms shall submit a capability statement not to exceed ten (10) pages, including: (1) company profile and 8(a) certification documentation; (2) evidence of compliance with FAR 52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting; (3) at least two (2) past performance references for custodial services in healthcare environments; and (4) DUNS/UEI number and SAM.gov registration verification.

This notice is for market research and planning purposes only. It does not constitute a solicitation and shall not be construed as a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

7.3.1-17 - Lowest Price Technically Acceptable Evaluation Method - November 2025
(a) After receipt and evaluation of proposals, FDIC may award a contract to the responsive offer that is the lowest priced and technically acceptable.
(b) After obtaining all pricing information, the FDIC may, at its discretion, limit the evaluation to the lowest priced offers or proposals. In such cases, additional proposals may not be evaluated. The FDIC also reserves the right to request additional price reductions from the lowest priced technically acceptable offeror, if it is in the FDIC's best interest.
(c) Technical acceptability will be determined by the offer meeting the FDIC's minimum technical requirements. Proposals will be evaluated as either "acceptable" or "unacceptable". If the offer cannot meet the minimum requirements of the solicitation, the offer will be deemed "unacceptable". There is no evaluation benefit for an offer that exceeds the minimum requirement.

SECTION M – EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

M.1 Basis for Award
The Government intends to award a single contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation is determined to be Technically Acceptable and offers the Lowest Price. The Government will evaluate quotations and may make award without discussions. Therefore, the offeror's initial quotation should contain its best terms.

M.2 Evaluation Approach
The Government will use a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. Technical quotations will be evaluated on a pass/fail basis. Price will not be evaluated for tradeoff against technical factors.

M.3 Technical Acceptability
To be rated Technically Acceptable, the quotation must clearly demonstrate compliance with all requirements of the solicitation, including:
- Relevant experience performing similar facility condition assessments
- Qualified personnel, including a licensed Professional Engineer (PE)
- Technical approach that meets all Statement of Work requirements
- Ability to meet the required schedule
Failure to meet any of the above criteria will result in a rating of Technically Unacceptable, and the quotation will not be considered for award.

M.4 Price Evaluation
Price will be evaluated for completeness and reasonableness in accordance with FAR 13.106-3.

Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.

Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

BASIS FOR AWARD
This acquisition is being conducted in accordance with FAR 13.5 along with procedures found within FAR 12 and FAR 15. This is a best value acquisition where the source selection process utilized is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA). Evaluation of quotations will be IAW FAR 52.212-2.
Following the LPTA evaluation process:
The Government will first evaluate price to determine the lowest through highest priced quotations. Only the lowest priced quote will be evaluated for technical acceptability, unless the lowest priced quote is deemed technically unacceptable. If the lowest priced quote is deemed technically unacceptable, the next lowest quote will be considered, and so on until a quoter is identified to be technically acceptable.
Contractors are advised that the Government may not evaluate the technical quotation response of all Contractors under this RFQ.

7.3.1-17 - Lowest Price Technically Acceptable Evaluation Method - November 2025
(a) After receipt and evaluation of proposals, FDIC may award a contract to the responsive offer that is the lowest priced and technically acceptable.
(b) After obtaining all pricing information, the FDIC may, at its discretion, limit the evaluation to the lowest priced offers or proposals. In such cases, additional proposals may not be evaluated. The FDIC also reserves the right to request additional price reductions from the lowest priced technically acceptable offeror, if it is in the FDIC's best interest.
(c) Technical acceptability will be determined by the offer meeting the FDIC's minimum technical requirements. Proposals will be evaluated as either 'acceptable' or 'unacceptable'. If the offer cannot meet the minimum requirements of the solicitation, the offer will be deemed 'unacceptable'. There is no evaluation benefit for an offer that exceeds the minimum requirement.

ADDENDUM TO 52.212-2
Paragraph (a) is hereby replaced with the following:
a. The Government will issue an award resulting from this solicitation to the responsible contractor whose quote meets or exceeds the minimum technical requirements of the solicitation and provides fair and reasonable pricing.
b. Award will be on an all or none basis. The following adjectival rating will be used in evaluating each offeror's technical capability quote:
Volume I - Factor 1 - Technical Capability: Technical capability ratings reflect the Government's confidence in each offeror's product, as demonstrated in its submission, to meet the salient characteristics set forth in the solicitation. The Government will make an independent judgment of the acceptability. To receive consideration for award, a rating of no less than Acceptable must be achieved in technical acceptability.
(1) ACCEPTABLE - To receive this rating, the offeror's product must meet the salient characteristics set forth in the solicitation.
(2) UNACCEPTABLE - An unacceptable rating will be assessed on any offeror that demonstrates any of the following: Failure to meet the Salient Characteristics set forth in the solicitation. Failure to provide sufficient detail for the Government to determine whether the items satisfactorily meet the requirements of this solicitation.
Volume II - Factor 2 - Price:
Quotes will be reviewed to determine them fair and reasonable and if accepted by the Contracting Officer, will be incorporated into the resulting contract. Offeror's pricing must be determined to be fair and reasonable in order to be eligible for an award.

SECTION M - EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD
In order to permit efficient competition and evaluation, the Navy may utilize the following methodology. Quotes will initially be assessed for price and ranked in order (lowest priced to highest priced). The Navy will then evaluate the technical factors of the lowest priced quote. In the event that this quote is deemed Unacceptable, the next lowest priced quote will be evaluated as many times as necessary, until the Government identifies a technically acceptable quote. The Navy will make award to the lowest priced technically acceptable quote, if possible. Accordingly, under this methodology, the technical factors of some quotes may not be evaluated by the Navy. The Government reserves the right to evaluate additional higher priced quoters to support a fair and reasonableness determination or for other considerations. If exchanges are deemed necessary by the Contracting Officer, a maximum of five (5) lowest priced quotes will be evaluated (both non-price and price) for the purpose of establishing a competitive range.

B. BASIS FOR AWARD (Section M)

The award will be made to a single responsible small business Offeror whose proposal is determined to be the lowest price, technically acceptable offer (FAR 15.101-2). To receive consideration for award, an "Acceptable" rating must be achieved for the Combined Technical/Management factor (to include all sub factors) and the Past Performance factor. The Cost/Price factor will not be rated, but will be evaluated for completeness, reasonableness and for realism as specified in this solicitation. Any proposal receiving a rating of "Unacceptable" in any non-price Factor will not be eligible for award. The evaluation criteria detailing the Factors and Subfactors are contained in Section M of the solicitation and are herein incorporated by reference.

The Government reserves the right to award without discussions; therefore, offerors will be cautioned to ensure that their proposals contain all required information and are complete in ALL respects.

FACTORS AND SUB-FACTORS TO BE EVALUATED

The following evaluation factors and sub factors will be used to evaluate each proposal. Award will be made based on the lowest price of the proposal that is deemed technically acceptable.

A detailed explanation of the criteria for evaluation is set forth in the "Evaluation Approach," paragraph C of this section. During evaluation of each proposal, the Government will assign each factor/sub factor, excluding Price, an Acceptable/Unacceptable rating. Each sub factor will be evaluated to determine if the proposed approach associated with each sub factor meets or does not meet the minimum performance or capability requirements. To receive an overall "Acceptable" factor rating, all sub factors must receive an "Acceptable" rating.

E.11 BASIS FOR AWARD

Award will be made to the lowest priced, responsive, responsible offeror whose quote conforms to the requirements of the solicitation set forth in the Price Schedule and Product Description. The total evaluated price shall be the sum of all proposed CLIN(s)/SLIN(s) set forth in Section B.4 Price Schedule of the RFQ.

All offerors are advised that, in the interest of efficiency, the Government reserves the right to conduct the evaluation in the most effective manner. Specifically, the Government may first evaluate the total proposed price of all offerors. Thereafter, the Government will evaluate the quote of the lowest priced offeror only. If the lowest priced offeror's quote is determined to conform to the requirements, the Government may make award to that offeror without further evaluation of the remaining offerors' quotes. If it is determined that the lowest priced offeror's quote does not conform to the requirement, then the Government may evaluate the next lowest priced quote and so forth and so on, until the Government reaches the lowest priced quote that is determined to conform to the requirement. However, the Government reserves the right to evaluate all offerors' quotes should it desire to conduct discussions or otherwise determine it to be in the Government's best interest.

Section M - Evaluation Factors for Award
52.212-2 ADDENDUM
BASIS FOR AWARD AND EVALUATION CRITERIA
This acquisition will be solicited, evaluated and awarded using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable, Source Selection, as supplemented by the Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS), and the Army Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (AFARS). A contract will be awarded to the Offeror deemed responsible in accordance with the FAR, as supplemented, whose proposal conforms to the solicitation's requirements and is judged the lowest price technically acceptable to the Government.
As a basis for award, trade-offs between price and non-price factors are not permitted. Offers received for less than the stated requirement will be considered ineligible for award. As set forth in FAR 52.215-1(f)(1), the Government intends to award a contract resulting from this solicitation to the responsible Offeror whose proposal represents the lowest price technically acceptable. Therefore, the Offeror's initial proposal should contain the Offeror's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. IAW FAR 15.408-2, discussions may be held. However, the Government may choose to award a contract without discussions. If discussions are determined unnecessary and award is made from initial proposals, proposals rated as 'Unacceptable' in Evaluation Factors 1 and/or 2 will not be considered for award.

7.3.1-17 - Lowest Price Technically Acceptable Evaluation Method - November 2025

(a) After receipt and evaluation of proposals, FDIC may award a contract to the responsive offer that is the lowest priced and technically acceptable.

(b) After obtaining all pricing information, the FDIC may, at its discretion, limit the evaluation to the lowest priced offers or proposals. In such cases, additional proposals may not be evaluated. The FDIC also reserves the right to request additional price reductions from the lowest priced technically acceptable offeror, if it is in the FDIC's best interest.

(c) Technical acceptability will be determined by the offer meeting the FDIC's minimum technical requirements. Proposals will be evaluated as either "acceptable" or "unacceptable". If the offer cannot meet the minimum requirements of the solicitation, the offer will be deemed "unacceptable". There is no evaluation benefit for an offer that exceeds the minimum requirement.

SECTION M – EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

M.1 Basis for Award
The Government intends to award a single contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation is determined to be Technically Acceptable and offers the Lowest Price. The Government will evaluate quotations and may make award without discussions. Therefore, the offeror's initial quotation should contain its best terms.

M.2 Evaluation Approach
The Government will use a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. Technical quotations will be evaluated on a pass/fail basis. Price will not be evaluated for tradeoff against technical factors.

M.3 Technical Acceptability
To be rated Technically Acceptable, the quotation must clearly demonstrate compliance with all requirements of the solicitation, including:
-Relevant experience performing similar facility condition assessments
-Qualified personnel, including a licensed Professional Engineer (PE)
-Technical approach that meets all Statement of Work requirements
-Ability to meet the required schedule
Failure to meet any of the above criteria will result in a rating of Technically Unacceptable, and the quotation will not be considered for award.

M.4 Price Evaluation
Price will be evaluated for completeness and reasonableness in accordance with FAR 13.106-3.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons (BOP) will utilize the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR Part 12. The Government intends to make a firm-fixed-unit price IDIQ award to the responsible business whose quotation provides the lowest price technically acceptable to the Government.
Proposals will be evaluated for technical acceptability based on the factors and sub-factors set forth below. No tradeoffs between price and non-price factors are permitted. A proposal must receive an "Acceptable" rating in every non-price factor to be eligible for award.
Definitions: For purposes of better understanding the decisions made based upon evaluation criteria selected for the Accreditation Services Contract for FY2026-2030 the evaluation terms for Technical have been defined as follows:
1.0 Technical Acceptability Definitions
For the purposes of this evaluation, the following definitions apply to all Technical Factors:
Acceptable: The proposal meets the minimum requirements of the solicitation and the Statement of Work.
Unacceptable: The proposal fails to meet the minimum requirements of the solicitation or contains deficiencies that increase the risk of unsuccessful contract performance to an unacceptable level.
2.2 Price Evaluation
Price will be the deciding factor among all proposals rated as "Technically Acceptable." The Government will evaluate price for:
1. Total Evaluated Price: Inclusion of the base period and all option years.
2. Reasonableness: Comparison of quotes to ensure the price is fair and reasonable.
3. Realism: Review to ensure the proposed price is not so low as to indicate a risk of performance failure.
Basis for Award: The Government intends to award a contract to the responsible offeror whose proposal is the lowest evaluated price and is technically Acceptable.

The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

Assumptions
1. Software Vendor: CMS intends to procure a SaaS based COTS platform, through a competitive process, to be configured and extended by the vendor in collaboration with CMS, MACs, and/or supporting contractors.
2. Professional Services: CMS envisions its primary role as policy development and oversight and expects to contract for operational services either as part of the software offering in a traditional SaaS model or with a software vendor and systems integrator team depending on the relationship of the services component to the software vendor.
3. Scale assumptions: ~1.2 billion claims per year (for sizing) and ~33.6 million beneficiaries in Original Medicare. CMS prefers a single scalable instance of vendor software supporting all beneficiaries; however, load may be distributed across an instance per MAC or MAC region if analytics, editing, and real-time program integrity have real-time cross-region awareness.
4. Stakeholder engagement: The contractor(s) are expected to coordinate closely with CMS, MACs, and Providers in workgroups, demos, requirements gathering and validation, training, and communications facilitated by the vendor and contractors in partnership with CMS.
5. Incremental delivery: Phased deployments including incremental run-out based roll outs including focused workstreams with defined scope, resources and timelines. Vendor as well as independent validation of outputs compared against legacy systems running in parallel will provide objective evidence of parity and reduce risk.

ClaimsCore contractors must engage with relevant CMS workgroups, offices, MACs, and partners through regular demos, open documentation, targeted training, standups, and efficient asynchronous updates (email, slack, Jira, etc.). Educational materials and hands on sandboxes should be provided to help build consensus on process especially where policy changes may be needed.

The tools referenced below are the CMS managed instances and will be furnished by CMS for the Contractor's use where relevant.
 Slack: Primary communication method; Shared channels for cross-team topics
 Confluence: All documentation will be stored here, including roadmaps, decision log, assumptions, meeting agendas and notes, research artifacts, team contact information, tech specs, etc.
 JIRA: Teams will utilize JIRA dashboard and reporting capabilities.
 GitHub: Single, open-source code repository. Contractors should expect to commit custom code developed for the government in a government owned GitHub. Where relevant, GitHub pull requests should be linked to JIRA user stories to connect the change to the requirements addressed.

Live data dashboards should be sourced from the Agile tools under this contract, or other resources as directed by the COR.

The nature of this action being approved is to bridge contract to the Agile Web Presence II (AWP II) Firm-Fixed Price (FFP) contract, GSA Number 47QTCA19D00CY, Contract number HM0476-20-F-0193, to extend the period of performance and increase the contract value. The scope for the AWP-II contract was determined to be necessary to support national security and to protect life and property, both in the continental United States and overseas. The current contract is set to expire on 29 March 2026 and an extension of the current contract is required to serve as a bridge until a follow-on competitive contract can be awarded. The AWP-II program provides continued operations and sustainment to the AWP web-based capability known as 'The Globe', an enterprise-wide, centralized web presence for discovery of NGA products, services, and tools, m.Globe, and Discovery Catalog on the Unclassified, NIPR, SIPR, JWICS, CAPNET, and Stoneghost domains within the IC. 'The Globe' collectively supports 178,000+ unique users across multiple security domains in their discovery of mission-critical geospatial intelligence for the Department of War (DoW), Intelligence Community (IC), National System for Geospatial-Intelligence (NSG), and Allied System for Geospatial-Intelligence (ASG) through the integration of geospatial programs and services. In accordance with FAR 8.405-6(a)(1)(i)(C), the required scope to the AWPII contract represents a logical follow-on to the original order in the interest of economy and efficiency. This requirement will have a Period of Performance (POP) that consistent of a 12-month Base Period and two (2) 6-month Optional Periods. Total POP will be for 24 months.

3.2 (U) Software Development
(U) The Contractor shall provide software development services to ensure the definition, design, implementation, and testing of the software to meet the functional and performance of new or augmented mission requirements while maximizing the effectiveness and efficiency. These new capabilities might include new sensor data integration or a new capability transitioned at "Initial Operational Capability (IOC)" that requires some development to reach "Full Operational Capability (FOC)" capability maturity. These services include the following:
• Sprint/Release planning
• Software coding
• Software integration
• Software unit testing and reporting (CDRL-A009, CDRL-A010)
• Software deployment to operations (CDRL-A008)
• Software enterprise/user testing

(U) Software development services shall, to the maximum extent possible, follow the established NGA Agile software development processes and procedures. This includes an Agile requirements management system using the current Scaled Agile Framework for all work performed, and the integration of Contractor development requirements (story and features) with the higher-level government requirements system (Program Epics and Solution Epics in USMS Jira on COE). 6.2.1 Subtask 1 – Product Backlog
The contractor shall develop and deliver an agile Product Backlog that is organized in a hierarchy where Epics are decomposed into Features and Features are decomposed into User Stories with bi-directional traceability between each level.

The contractor shall collaborate with key stakeholders, including the Government Product Owner to elicit, document, refine, and prioritize backlog items. Backlog items shall contain, at a minimum, the requisite information:
• Title
• Description
• Level of Effort (e.g., story points, hours, etc.)
• Definition of Done / Acceptance Criteria
• Dependencies
• Other attributes that characterize the story

Stories shall fall into one of four states:
• Proposed: The story is complete (contains the required attributes) but has not been approved by the Government Product Owner.
• Approved: The story has been approved, and work can begin.
• In-Progress: The story is approved, assigned to a team, and is currently under development.
• Done: The story has been completed (meeting the definition of done and all acceptance criteria) and is accepted by the Government Product Owner.

The contractor shall employ agile backlog grooming practices, including backlog refinement sessions, to continuously review, update, and reprioritize items based on stakeholder feedback, technical feasibility, and business value. The backlog shall be maintained in an agile project management tool designated by the Government.

THE NGA SOFTWARE WAY - EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

SOFTWARE METRICS
To deliver on its current and future mission, NGA needs to deliver useful software faster and more consistently. To measure progress toward this intent, NGA will track three key metrics for each software product: availability, lead time for changes, and deployment frequency. Additionally, each software product is expected to have its own product-specific metrics to track how well the product is working for its users. Together, these metrics balance speed and reliability and describe a product's impact and value.

The metrics and elements in this document, collectively referred to as "The NGA Software Way," serve as an implementation guide to supplement NGA's Technology Strategy and provide additional specific guidance for teams building and operating software.

For software teams using NGA's enterprise-managed version control and Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipeline, deployment frequency and lead time for changes will be automatically tracked within those services' dashboards. Teams must prioritize rapidly getting a product to actual users in production to learn what works and how to successfully iterate; this first capability must be delivered in months, not years. DORA research shows high-performing teams deploy at least once per week.

3. Prioritize Rapidly Delivering Value to Users
Once user needs are deeply understood, the team must decide which of those needs to prioritize. To do this, the team must define a minimum viable product (MVP), either in written form or, ideally, as a set of wireframes, and then share it with key stakeholders, customers, and users. The MVP is the smallest actual deliverable that can be reviewed by real customers to get feedback on if it solves a need and to test assumptions, learn quickly, and decrease risk upfront. The product manager must break down the MVP into actionable work items that are easily understood by the entire team. Teams must gather feedback as early as possible using mockups or interactive prototypes. As feedback is gathered, the prototype's fidelity should mature, and the team must transition to testing with the real product.

Each day a product isn't actively solving user needs in production increases the risk the team is building the wrong thing. Teams must prioritize rapidly getting a product to actual users in production to learn what works and how to successfully iterate; this first capability must be delivered in months, not years. If the initially defined MVP cannot be delivered to real users in production within single-digit months, it must be broken down further.
agile_methodology

13. Iterate and Improve Continually
Products and services are never "finished." Getting real people using your product as early as possible, monitoring the product's performance, and making improvements throughout the lifetime of the product enable teams to adapt quickly. Making improvements means more than doing basic maintenance like fixing defects in code, deploying security patches, and keeping runbooks up to date; continual improvement means you can capture and respond to changes in user needs, technology, or government policy throughout the lifetime of the product.
Iteration isn't just for the early stages of a product's development; a team must be able to make substantial improvements throughout a product's lifetime. As product complexity grows, teams must focus on balancing paying down technical debt with new feature development, ensuring the product continues to be adaptable over time to respond rapidly to user needs.
Questions to Answer
► How has your product evolved in the past six months? ► What is your usability testing process and cadence?
► What are the biggest upcoming changes, and why are ► What changes have you made to the product based on they needed? usability testing?
► Can you share your product roadmap?
CONCLUSION
The above metrics and 13 elements, collectively referred to as "The NGA Software Way," ensure each NGA software product has the necessary people, processes, and tools in place to successfully deliver. Together, they enable NGA to consistently build, release, and operate software that meets our users' needs and expectations and deliver on our mission.

2. Deeply Understand User Needs
Before beginning technical work, a product manager and team must engage with real and/or expected users to identify and understand users' underlying needs, as well as learn how the product or service will fit into the users' work. This is not just a government product manager (or anyone else) hearing users say what they "want." It requires deeply understanding their mission and goals as well as current approaches. Only by understanding the existing state and the reasoning behind it can the team design and engineer a solution.

Human-centered design involves engaging with users to understand their problems and concerns. Methods for doing so include interviews, usability testing, and prototyping. The identified user needs, including KPIs and key findings, must be regularly shared with stakeholders and kept in NGA's enterprise-managed collaboration tools.

3. Prioritize Rapidly Delivering Value to Users
Teams must define a minimum viable product (MVP), either in written form or, ideally, as a set of wireframes, and then share it with key stakeholders, customers, and users. The MVP is the smallest actual deliverable that can be reviewed by real customers to get feedback on if it solves a need and to test assumptions, learn quickly, and decrease risk upfront. Teams must gather feedback as early as possible using mockups or interactive prototypes. As feedback is gathered, the prototype's fidelity should mature, and the team must transition to testing with the real product. Market research must be conducted to understand if a solution already exists inside or outside of NGA that sufficiently meets user needs or can be easily altered to do so. Only after thorough analysis of the existing landscape can software product teams confidently determine whether to buy, augment, or build a solution to address user needs.

VETERAN FOCUSED INTEGRATION PROCESS (VIP) AND PRODUCT LINE MANAGEMENT (PLM)

The Contractor shall support VA efforts IAW the updated Veteran-Focused Integration Process (VIP) and Product Line Management (PLM) Playbook. The major focus of VIP is on Enterprise Oversight, Governance, reporting, and establishing practices that focus on outcomes and continuously delivering sustainable IT capabilities to benefit the Veteran. PLM is a framework that focuses on delivering functional products that provide the highest priority work to customers while delivering simplified, reliable, and practical solutions to the business, medical staff, and our Veterans. The VIP Guide is a companion guide to the PLM Transformation Playbook and can be found at: https://www.voa.va.gov/DocumentView.aspx?DocumentID=4371 and the PLM Transformation Playbook can be found at https://www.voa.va.gov/DocumentView.aspx?DocumentID=4946. VIP and PLM are the authoritative processes that IT projects must follow to ensure development and delivery of IT products.

The PLM Transformation Playbook details how OIT leadership expects Product Lines (PLs) to implement PLM, DevOps, and Lean-Agile principles, methods, practices, and techniques through levels of maturity. The PLM Transformation Playbook applies to all Information Technology (IT) products aligned to PLs in the Software Product Management, Infrastructure Operations and Product Engineering organizations within the OIT organization.

C.3.8.4 Software Development and Technical Labor Support

The contractor shall provide Software Development and Technical Labor Support for the NextGen Passport Personalization Printers within the Non-Production Environment (NPE), passport operations, and support for CA service desk tickets. 

The contractor shall: 

Provide support for the Print Controller software to successfully communicate with the TDIS. 

Develop and support the application to enable Passport staff to perform Government Acceptance Testing (GAT) for personalization printers in a standalone environment independent of the Print Controller and TDIS. The application will be able to accept the passport print jobs in XML format, perform chip and coding, engrave on the personalization page, and print the endorsement page.

Work with the Government to establish virtual development and testing environments on the NPE.

Fix performance, security, and code issues identified by the Government in NPE. The Government plans to use static code analysis to identify issues and will work with the Contractor to determine required fixes before moving to IV&V.

Design and develop new functions based on new user stories, e.g., encryption, page refresh, and labeling.

Develop comprehensive test cases and test scenarios, and perform end to end integration, usability and performance testing working with the support site location team to ensure robustness of software, and highest quality and accurate production of passport books.

Assist in supporting GitLab updates, logging defects in ELM, and work with CA Security in scanning and remediation, and enhancing any user level documentation.
